description: Machine-readable contracts for the 48 Hoffman deck layouts (L01–L51). A layout code is a CONTRACT, not a descriptive tag: if a slide is labelled L03/L20/L35/L37, it must preserve that layout's element relationships, hierarchy, slot roles and defining visual behaviour. If the content doesn't fit, pick a different layout — never invent a new composition under an existing code. The directly-editable visual source of truth is `templates/deck/Deck.dc.html`; this JSON is for tools that can't consume that DC (e.g. python-pptx generators). Read alongside `LAYOUTS.md` (prose) and `assets/asset-manifest.json` (logo geometry).

safeArea.note: All text + non-bleed imagery stays inside. Page chrome (page number, logo) lives inside this margin.

title.note: bias 72–80; emphasis (key word or phrase) in Libre Baskerville italic

cover.note: size-to-fit; grow to fill on 1–2 lines

rules.headline: ≤ 8 words, editorial not descriptive, italic emphasis on the key word or phrase (one emphasis per line, chosen by meaning not count)
rules.connectors: Every arrow connects two NAMED elements edge-to-edge or edge-to-centreline; never terminates in open space; never an oversized arrow glyph floating between text boxes. Build arrows in the same layout pass as their nodes; endpoints track the visible node/text, not an oversized textbox bounding box.
rules.logo: Choose variant from surface via assets/asset-manifest.json > surfaceToLogo. Lock aspect ratio; derive one dimension from the other.
rules.protectedZones: Keep all content out of the wordmark + boxed-monogram bounding box + clearspace (asset-manifest.json > protectedZones). The full-frame storyline line is a background layer, not a protected zone.

modeLegend.presenter: live, ≤15 words/slide, detail in speaker notes
modeLegend.both: works in either mode at its own density

layouts.name: Cover
layouts.onePoint: The deck's single idea as a huge headline.

slots.name: title
slots.role: giant headline, italic emphasis (key word or phrase)

layouts.keepHonest: No agenda, no body copy.

layouts.name: Cover (spectrum bar)
layouts.onePoint: One idea, huge; a full-height spectrum bar flush left carries brand energy.

slots.name: title
slots.role: ≤8 words, italic emphasis (key word or phrase), 130px+

layouts.keepHonest: The bar is the only ornament. No body copy.

layouts.name: Section divider
layouts.onePoint: The section name set huge; the number is the second voice.

slots.name: title
slots.role: big section title, italic emphasis (key word or phrase)

slots.name: motif
slots.role: optional small graphic / one Fluent emoji

layouts.keepHonest: One or two words. Never a sentence, never bullets. NOT a holding slide for workflow content — that is L35.

layouts.name: Section divider (spectrum bar)
layouts.onePoint: Section name huge; mono number the second voice; edge bar instead of colour flood.
layouts.keepHonest: One or two words, never a sentence.

layouts.name: Agenda
layouts.onePoint: The shape of the journey — 3–5 numbered items.
layouts.keepHonest: Titles only, no descriptions. 3–5 items. CRITICAL: the numbered item rows must NOT overlap the boxed Storyline monogram in the corner — compute the monogram's protected rect and keep rows clear of it (this is the exact failure from the diagnostic deck).

layouts.name: Statement + support
layouts.onePoint: The claim, italic emphasis (key word or phrase).
layouts.keepHonest: Support extends the headline. No second paragraph.

layouts.name: Big-idea split
layouts.onePoint: A provocative claim (left) with an emotive image (right).
layouts.keepHonest: Role box is ONE line. One setup paragraph only.

layouts.name: Punchline (giant word)
layouts.onePoint: One word ENORMOUS; setup is a whisper before the shout.
layouts.keepHonest: One word is the whole slide.

layouts.name: Two-beat statement
layouts.onePoint: Setup → turn: two short phrases; the hinge word is the point.
layouts.keepHonest: Two short beats, nothing between. Hinge word in white; rest lime/aqua. NOT two rounded panels — it is two beats of type on ONE surface.

layouts.name: Centered motif opener
layouts.onePoint: One Fluent emoji sets the mood; the headline lands the line.

slots.name: motif
slots.role: 1 Fluent emoji size 120–200 (or a short row)

layouts.keepHonest: One emoji or one tight row — never a wall.

layouts.name: Script + acronym opener
layouts.onePoint: A big acronym IS the slide; a small multicolor script word adds warmth.
layouts.keepHonest: The script move is the house voice — use once. The acronym carries everything.

layouts.name: Plan-on-a-page matrix
layouts.onePoint: The strategy line in the banner; the grid proves it's coherent.
layouts.keepHonest: Cells are labels, not sentences. Reference artefact — exactly one per deck. Body may drop to 24–28px because the table is the point.

layouts.name: Orbit / ring overview
layouts.onePoint: One centre, six equal parts, each an icon above a label.
layouts.keepHonest: Six parts, equal weight. Labels ≤3 words.

layouts.name: Highlight box + arrows (before → after)
layouts.onePoint: Flow reads left-to-right; the highlighted middle box is the idea. ALSO the default source → tool → output workflow layout.

slots.name: boxStart
slots.role: plain start node: icon + ≤4-word title + one line

slots.name: boxHighlight
slots.role: THE key concept, accent border + lime tint

slots.name: arrows
slots.note: Arrows are horizontal, anchored edge-to-edge between the three nodes. For a source → AI tool → output workflow, this is the canonical layout: source left, tool highlighted center, output right, arrows between node edges, one consistent geometry reused across all demo/holding slides. Arrows MUST NOT float in open space.

layouts.keepHonest: Exactly one box highlighted. Three boxes, one line each — not a flowchart. NOT three rounded cards with a floating down-arrow.

layouts.name: Three-column image cards
layouts.onePoint: The pattern across the columns; each column is one beat.
layouts.keepHonest: 3 columns max, equal weight.

layouts.name: Three-column textured icon cards
layouts.onePoint: The pattern across three verticals; two market bullets prove each.
layouts.keepHonest: Two market bullets per column, not three.

layouts.name: Three audiences ('we want them to…')
layouts.onePoint: The set of audiences and the behaviour change per audience.
layouts.keepHonest: The 'want' card is the payload — one sentence.

layouts.name: Numbered points (vertical rules)
layouts.onePoint: The set read across; the big mono number paces it, one line each.
layouts.keepHonest: RULES, not boxes — keep it light. One line per point. NOT rounded cards.

layouts.name: Persona profile
layouts.onePoint: Who they are and the one job they need done.
layouts.keepHonest: Two quotes max, in their voice.

layouts.name: Team roster
layouts.onePoint: The shape and seniority of the team.

slots.name: groups
slots.role: grouped columns; each: portrait + name + role + ≤6-word remit

layouts.keepHonest: Name + role + one remit line. No bios (that's L16).

layouts.name: Single bio
layouts.onePoint: Why this person earns the room's trust.
layouts.keepHonest: Lead with the most credible fact.

layouts.name: Idea detail
layouts.onePoint: The idea name + its single mechanic.
layouts.keepHonest: Lists ≤3 items, ≤6 words each.

layouts.name: Two-story side-by-side
layouts.onePoint: Two distinct angles serving one theme.
layouts.keepHonest: Two stories, equal weight.

layouts.name: Ongoing story engagement
layouts.onePoint: Two parallel story pitches, equal weight, for one audience.

slots.name: cards
slots.note: one white card + one navy card + illustration on rail

layouts.keepHonest: Two cards, equal weight; each names its target media.

layouts.name: Story idea + role + image
layouts.onePoint: The angle headline + one-line role; the image names the idea.

slots.name: roleBox
slots.role: navy, ≤30 words, ONE line of role

slots.name: imageCaption
slots.role: the idea name only, ≤4 words, lime caption bar

layouts.keepHonest: Role box is one line. Caption is the idea's name only.

layouts.name: Stat strip
layouts.onePoint: The result. The biggest number matters most.
layouts.keepHonest: Max 3 stats. Prefer one hero + two context.

layouts.name: Cascade stat boxes
layouts.onePoint: Three results stacked as one cascading form.
layouts.keepHonest: Three boxes, three numbers. Don't add a fourth.

layouts.name: Stat grid (light slide)
layouts.onePoint: The spread of evidence; the grid reads as a rhythm.
layouts.keepHonest: Equal weight across cells — no hero here (that's L19).

layouts.name: Data dashboard
layouts.onePoint: The headline finding big; the breakdown supports it.
layouts.keepHonest: One headline finding leads. No chart junk, no legend essays.

layouts.name: Timeline / measurement arc
layouts.onePoint: The progression or the set of dimensions.
layouts.keepHonest: 4–6 nodes ALONG the arc (not three circles with generic arrows). Notes are captions, not paragraphs.

layouts.name: Process / journey diagram
layouts.onePoint: The model's name and its cyclical/holistic shape.
layouts.keepHonest: Name each stage; don't explain it on the slide.

layouts.name: Scope / deliverables table
layouts.onePoint: The breadth and cadence of what's committed.

slots.name: table
slots.role: deliverable rows × market columns, counts/ticks, navy header row, hairline grid

layouts.keepHonest: A true reference table — the one place dense text is correct. Body may drop to 24–28px.

layouts.name: Icon benefit grid
layouts.onePoint: The sum — 'here's why us.'
layouts.keepHonest: Titles ≤4 words. Long copy kills the rhythm.

layouts.name: Capability grid
layouts.onePoint: The breadth of proprietary tools.
layouts.keepHonest: Names are the stars; descriptors one line.

layouts.name: Pill rows + icon-circles
layouts.onePoint: The set of moves; each row is one.
layouts.keepHonest: One line per row. Highlight one row, not all.

layouts.name: Photo + numbered lists
layouts.onePoint: The themes (numbered) and their proof (bullets).
layouts.keepHonest: Photo is half the slide. ≤3 bullets/section, ≤7 words each.

layouts.name: Three photo + bullets
layouts.onePoint: Three efforts, equal weight; photo makes each real.
layouts.keepHonest: Three columns, equal weight. Bullets ≤8 words.

layouts.name: Pain ↔ Solutions
layouts.onePoint: Every pain has a matching answer.
layouts.keepHonest: Pair 1:1. Pains in the client's voice, short.

layouts.name: Map + narrative
layouts.onePoint: 'The right network, not the biggest.'

slots.name: officeList
slots.role: regional columns (texture, not a reading task)

layouts.keepHonest: The narrative carries the argument; the city list is texture.

layouts.name: Values statement
layouts.onePoint: The relationship you're promising.
layouts.keepHonest: 3–4 values, one line each. No nested bullets.

layouts.name: Full-bleed image + overlay
layouts.onePoint: The headline over the image; the image is the emotion.
layouts.keepHonest: One line of type. Needs a paragraph? It's not a full-bleed.

layouts.name: Split 50/50
layouts.onePoint: The claim (one half); the image (other half).
layouts.keepHonest: Don't fill the type half to the edges — keep air.

layouts.name: Pull quote
layouts.onePoint: The quote; one phrase highlighted.
layouts.keepHonest: One quote, ≤20 words. Highlight one phrase.

layouts.name: Bento collage (4)
layouts.onePoint: The set as one textured collage; the largest cell carries the hero.

slots.name: cells
slots.note: mixed aspects into rounded cells, tight gaps, fill biggest first

layouts.keepHonest: One cell leads, the rest support. Not all equal.

layouts.name: Bento collage (5)
layouts.onePoint: Five images as one composed object.
layouts.keepHonest: Mixed cell sizes give the rhythm.

layouts.name: Bento collage (7)
layouts.onePoint: Seven images as one composed object.
layouts.keepHonest: Don't make every cell equal.

layouts.name: Closing
layouts.onePoint: The invitation — a short imperative.
layouts.keepHonest: A verb + a way to reach you. No recap, no bullets.

layouts.name: Q&A / hands closer
layouts.onePoint: An invitation to talk; the emoji carry the energy.

slots.name: emojiRow
slots.role: raised hands or faces along the base

layouts.keepHonest: One short label + a row of emoji. No agenda recap.

note_on_coverage: All 48 catalogued layouts are represented. `coreForKnowledgeWorkers` lists the 11 the brief flagged as most-used (cover, agenda, section divider, insight/statement, north-star/motif opener, image+text split, two-beat comparison, three numbered points, source→tool→output workflow, key takeaways/stat strip, closing). Coordinate boxes are provided for the geometrically-defined layouts; for the rest, slot roles + type tiers + word caps ARE the contract. Bounding boxes are guides in the 1920×1080 space, inside the 72px safe area unless the slot is explicitly full-bleed.
